message:
chore(beads): file 7k7pl (meta — presence-only asserts sweep-and-upgrade plan)

Records the systemic finding surfaced by the targeted hardening
sweep in commits 0336ca4b / 6227d21d / e191d9f1 / b1a99590: at
least 13 test files carry `.is_some()`, 15 carry `!is_empty()`,
10 carry single-token `.contains(...)` assertions that pass
under regressions which the shipped pattern upgrades would catch.
Bead captures the categorization + upgrade recipe + cost estimate
so a future sweep can pick up where this cycle left off.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-7k7pl","title":"[MEDIUM] meta: systematic presence-only test asserts across tests/ — sweep-and-upgrade plan","description":"PATTERN FINDING from targeted test-hardening sweep (commits 0336ca4b, 6227d21d, e191d9f1, b1a99590).

Upgraded 8 presence-only assertions across 4 test files to pin content+shape. Broader scan shows the pattern is systemic:

- `assert!(X.is_some())` with no follow-up unwrap+equality check: at least 13 distinct test files carry this pattern, spread across connector_*.rs (amp, claude, codex, copilot_cli, gemini, opencode), storage*.rs, pages_wizard.rs, secret_scan.rs, search_asset_harness.rs.
- `assert!(!X.is_empty())` with no count or content pin: at least 15 test files.
- `assert!(X.contains("single_token"))` without checking the enclosing line structure: at least 10 test files.

These assertions look like they pass when the code under test is correct but ALSO pass when a regression has quietly degraded the output shape — a fresh`None` that used to be `Some(expected)`, a scanned batch that dropped to a single result, an event string that no longer includes the accompanying fields.

EXAMPLES of the upgrade pattern already shipped as reference:
- 0336ca4b: `assert!(mode.is_some())` → `assert_eq!(mode, Some(SearchMode::Semantic), "...")`
- 6227d21d: two independent `out.contains(...)` probes → find single line + assert both fields on THAT line
- e191d9f1: `!manifest.files.is_empty()` → `>= 15 AND contains(specific_asset_name)` with diagnostic
- b1a99590: `metadata.len() > 0` → `>= 1024 bytes` with named floor

PROPOSED APPROACH:
1. Batch-review each of the three pattern categories across the identified test files, one per commit.
2. For `.is_some()` on enum-typed Options: always pin the expected variant via `assert_eq!(x, Some(Variant))`.
3. For `!is_empty()`: pin a concrete lower bound (`>= N`) AND at least one expected element by name/content.
4. For `contains(single_token)`: if two or more `.contains()` probes are expected to come from the same event, rewrite to find the event line first and assert all fields on that line.
5. Keep diagnostics specific ("got {N} entries: {:?}") so failure mode is immediately actionable.

Estimated cost: ~15 test files x 20 min/file (identify candidates, upgrade, verify) = 5 hours of careful work spread across multiple panes. Not critical path — the existing tests already fire on most regressions, but the upgrades close silent-pass windows.

Labels: testing, code-quality, hardening.
Source commits of reference upgrades: 0336ca4b, 6227d21d, e191d9f1, b1a99590.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:42:34.381384257Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:42:34.381384257Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-7kj5","title":"T5.3: Performance regression detection","description":"Add automated performance regression detection.

## Implementation
1. Store baseline metrics
2. Compare current run to baseline
3. Alert on significant regressions
4. Track trends over time

## Metrics to Track
- Test suite duration
- Search latency (P50, P95)
- Indexing throughput
- Memory usage peaks

## Thresholds
- Duration: >20% regression alerts
- Latency: >10% regression alerts
- Memory: >15% regression alerts

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Baseline storage implemented
- [ ] Comparison logic works
- [ ] Alerts on regressions
- [ ] Trend visualization","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T04:24:27.778064Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T06:47:44.323364Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T06:47:44.323279Z","close_reason":"Implemented metric-specific thresholds (10% latency, 20% duration, 15% memory, 10% throughput), historical trend tracking, trend analysis, updated bench.yml workflow","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
